judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports nail the gateway boundary violations, the missing external_ref field, and the timing of the doc fix, but Report 1 is better overall because it correctly recommends removing the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the source of truth, whereas Report 2 just bumps "4" to "5" and re-enshrines the same drift-prone claim.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should go in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`, alongside the existing `charge()` and `create_customer()` accessors ... I found two places, beyond the documented `errors.py` exception, that construct their own `chargekit.Client` and call the provider directly instead of going through `gateway.py`: - `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4` ... - `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2`

report_2.evidence: New calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... I found two places that talk to the provider a different way, both instantiating their own `chargekit.Client` instead of using `gateway.py`: - `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4` and `:14` ... - `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2` and `:9` ... The only sanctioned exception is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`

report_1.evidence: "The Invoice API response contains exactly the fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status." False as stated — `InvoiceOut` (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is included in every response built via `InvoiceOut.from_model()` ... This field was added in migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_2.evidence: claims the response "contains exactly the fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status" (5 fields). The actual `InvoiceOut` dataclass (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) now has 6 fields — the same 5 plus `external_ref`.

report_1.evidence: drift between the contract doc and the code is a bug, not a "someday" cleanup item, and any change to a documented invariant must update the doc in the same commit as the code change. Concretely, before or as part of adding refund support: ... Going forward, any commit that adds a refund accessor to `gateway.py`, or touches `InvoiceOut`, or adds/removes a webhook branch, must update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` in that same commit

report_2.evidence: It needs two corrections, both overdue rather than hypothetical future work ... Timing: this should happen now, as part of the refund PR (or immediately before it) rather than deferred ... Fixing the code drift (routing all calls through `gateway.py`) should happen in the same change so the doc doesn't need a second correction right after this one; if that refactor is out of scope, update the doc text alone as a small, immediate PR

report_1.evidence: rather than just correcting "4" to "5" and adding `payment_pending` (a fix that will silently go stale again the next time someone adds a branch — the exact failure mode that just happened), state the invariant so it can't drift unnoticed, per project documentation's "don't cite a number, read the source" guidance: either point at the source of truth ("see the `kind ==` branches in `app/api/webhooks.py` for the current list of handled event types")
